content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 5, 1990

Robert Eich, Executive Director
Commission on State Mandates
1414 K Street, Suite 315
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-531

Dear Mr. Eich:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

Are designees for the members of the Commission on State
Mandates required to file Statements of Economic Interests (Form
730)?

CONCLUSION

Each designee must file statements of economic interests.
The disclosure category for designees is the same as for the
members.

FACTS

The Commission on State Mandates is comprised of the Director
of Finance, the State Treasurer, the State Controller, the
Director of the Office of Planning and Research and a public
member. It is common practice for some of these members to send
designees to the Commission hearings. The designee has full
voting privileges and decision-making authority.

1/ Government Code Section 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Robert Eich
page 2

ANALYSIS

The Political Reform Act requires governmental agencies to
adopt a conflict of interest code. (Section 87300.) The code
must designate those positions within the agency which may or
participate in the making of decisions which may have a
foreseeable material financial effect on any financial interest
and must require persons whose positions are designated in the
code to file periodic statements of economic interests (Section
87302.). For each designated position, the code must describe the
types of financial interests made reportable. (Section 87302.)

The conflict of interest code for the Commission on State
Mandates prescribes its members, executive director, and legal
counsel to disclose all investments, interests in real property,
and income from any source located in or doing business in the
State of California.

The designees, when sitting as voting members of the
Commission, make or participate in the making of decisions and
therefore are required to file statements of economic interests.
The designees do not act as proxies for the members by merely
complying with the directions of the members, but rather formulate
their own opinions and vote on matters in accordance with their
own beliefs and views.

Since it is often difficult to know when each designee will
attend a particular meeting, each designee must file an assuming
office statement at the time of his or her appointment. The
designee should continue to file annual statements until such time
as he or she no longer acts as a designee. Then, a leaving office
statement must be filed within 30 days. (Klein Advice Letter
A-88-232, copy enclosed.)

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

by: Colleen McGee
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division
